title: The Unit Impulse Function δ(t)
summary: This page introduces the unit impulse function δ(t), defined by Dirac as zero everywhere except at t=0 with unit area under the curve. It explains the impulse as a limiting case of a tall, narrow rectangular pulse with infinitesimally small width and infinitely large height but unit area. The page also includes Drill 1.8, which demonstrates how a piecewise signal can be expressed using unit step functions.

figures.caption: A piecewise signal x(t) that ramps linearly from 0 to 1 between t=1 and t=2, remains at 1 from t=2 to t=4, and is zero elsewhere.

The Dirac delta function δ(t) shown symbolically in (a) and represented in (b) as the limit of a rectangular pulse of width ε and height 1/ε as ε→0, illustrating one common approximation of the impulse function.
